behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Diff parsing and line-number extraction are common sources of off-by-one and context errors. A mocked diff with valid format hides parsing bugs, incorrect line mapping, and improper scope handling. Testing requires real diff data structures and real parsing logic to verify that only changed lines are extracted and passed to the analysis agent.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test PR with a multi-file change (add new function, modify existing function). Verify that the diff is correctly parsed into changed line ranges, and that the agent receives only the diff context, not the full file contents. Mock the downstream analysis agent but verify the diff input matches the expected changed lines.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: GitHub's API validates comment position metadata (commit, file path, line number, side of diff). An in-process mock would accept invalid positions that GitHub rejects. The failure boundary is real GitHub API validation and UI rendering. Wrong positions cause comments to appear on unintended code or be rejected entirely, which only real GitHub API interaction can catch.
behaviors.test_description: Post inline comments to a real (or test) GitHub pull request and verify they appear at the intended line numbers and file paths in the GitHub UI. Include edge cases: comments at file boundaries, on added vs removed lines, and on different files in a multi-file change. Verify that attempts to post to invalid positions are rejected by GitHub API.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on what the real LLM returns when analyzing code. A mocked agent that returns 'bug_found=True' hides the real failure: the LLM fails to detect the bug. Bug detection is non-deterministic across models, versions, and runs. The failure is not in the orchestration but in whether the real model can identify real defects in real code.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test pull request with a known, intentional bug (e.g., missing null check, incorrect condition, logic error). Run the real code review agent against it and verify that the agent reports at least one finding that correctly identifies the bug. Run multiple times to account for LLM non-determinism if needed.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to an inline comment that was also posted — no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment.
behaviors.justification: This verifies consistency between two outputs (inline comments and summary) that are generated from the same findings list. If findings are filtered, mutated, or fetched separately between steps, Unit-level tests of each function in isolation would miss orchestration bugs. Need to test that the summary generation and inline comment posting logic both operate over the same finding set with real finding objects and real generation logic.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full comment-posting workflow with a test set of findings (mocked from the agent). Collect the posted inline comments and the generated summary. Verify that every finding in the summary text appears as at least one inline comment, and vice versa. Check for edge cases: empty findings, single finding, duplicate findings, and findings with special characters or line breaks.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — reading the diff, posting inline comments, posting the summary comment — completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end test of three sequential steps with multiple external dependencies: diff reading (real PR data), inline comment posting (GitHub API), and summary posting (GitHub API), plus the agentic analysis step (real LLM). Mocks at any point (GitHub API or LLM) hide real failures: API timeouts, malformed responses, LLM failures, state mutations between steps, or format mismatches. The failure boundary spans multiple real-world systems in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real pull request (or use a test PR on a real GitHub repository). Run the full automation pipeline end-to-end without mocks: read the real diff, invoke the real LLM for analysis, post inline comments to the real PR, and post the summary comment. Verify that all steps complete successfully and in the correct order. Verify that inline comments and summary are visible on the PR. Test failure recovery: what happens if GitHub API is temporarily unavailable, LLM times out, or PR data is malformed.
